label: Hardware Insufficiency — Upgrade Required
thinkingSteps: Retrieving device snapshot from 30 minutes ago…
Analyzing usage patterns for the past 3 months…
Comparing your usage against similar HP EliteBook 840 G8 devices…
Checking restart history and RAM recovery patterns…

diagnosticRows.label: p90_memory_utilization = 96.2%  (threshold: > 85%, sustained 27 / 30 days)
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ RAM consistently maxed — not a one-time spike

diagnosticRows.label: avg_processor_time = 27.4%  while  p90_memory_utilization = 96.2%
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ CPU idle while RAM maxed — active swap thrashing

diagnosticRows.label: restart events: 8 in 30 days · RAM climbs back to 90%+ within 3–5 days each time
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ Pattern: restarts provide temporary relief but RAM creeps back

diagnosticRows.label: memory_size_gb = 16 · Engineering role typical demand: 28–30 GB
p90_boot_time = 68s  (threshold: < 30s)
diagnosticRows.result: ⚠️ Slow boot — swap activity causing storage bottleneck

rootCauses.label: Hardware Capacity Mismatch
rootCauses.detail: 16 GB RAM is insufficient for an Engineering role
rootCauses.evidence: Engineering role typically demands 28–30 GB RAM. With memory_size_gb = 16, the OS continuously pages to disk. avg_processor_time = 27.4% while p90_memory_utilization = 96.2% — the CPU is idle waiting on disk swaps. Jordan restarts 8 times per month but RAM climbs back within days — this confirms the issue is structural, not behavioral. Only a hardware upgrade resolves it.
rootCauses.benchmark: RAM pressure is higher than 93% of HP EliteBook 840 G8 devices in your org

rootCauses.label: Memory Thrashing — Swap Storm
rootCauses.evidence: In normal operation, CPU and memory utilization rise together. Here avg_processor_time (27.4%) is paradoxically low while RAM is maxed — the processor is stalled waiting for disk paging. This swap storm pattern recurs after every restart, confirming the root cause is hardware insufficiency, not usage behavior.
rootCauses.benchmark: CPU-to-memory divergence is higher than 91% of 16 GB laptops in your org

benchmarkSummary.interpretation: Jordan's laptop consistently uses more RAM than 93% of similar HP EliteBook 840 G8 devices. The pattern of frequent restarts with RAM climbing back each time indicates the 16 GB spec is structurally insufficient for this Engineering role — not a fixable usage habit.

upgrade.title: Request a Team-Purchased Laptop
upgrade.body:
Based on your usage patterns and Engineering role, a 32 GB / 1 TB laptop has been pre-approved for your team budget.

  • Submit hardware upgrade request via IT Portal →

Expected processing: 5–7 business days. You'll receive a confirmation email once approved.
upgrade.source: Source: IT Equipment Policy · Team Budget Database · Job Role Access Database

inperson.title: Visit IT Support Office
inperson.body:
Bring your current laptop for an in-person assessment and same-day replacement if stock is available.

  • 📍 Building 3, Floor 2 — IT Support Office
  • 🕐 Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM
  • 🪪 Bring your employee badge
